entry.title: Re: XBLNR Down payment request
entry.content:
Thank you for your request, @ACALVO .

In reports, automatic payments or payment advices, the alphanumeric reference document number, XBLNR, provided on supplier invoices or payment requests, may be referred to as simple text, e.g. as "Payment Reference", "Your invoice", "Your reference ID", etc.

If this helped answer your request, please mark this response accordingly. Thank you.

entry.title: IBP - Last Value in Period when Mid-Period
entry.content:
We are trying to show the last value in a period and this works for all but the current period - example attached of what we are trying to achieve.

The KF is stored at a technical week level and is being reported at a month level. The issue we are having is that for the current month, nothing is reported as the last technical week of that period is still NULL when we are mid-month. During the current month, we would like to report the current technical week's value.

We tried using IBP_LPA, but this resulted in a NULL current month as described above.

We also tried using IBP_LAST_VALUE, but due to having to choose an aggregation level for the REQUEST level, we could not get the result to be correct (for example, if we used MAX, it chose the MAX value over the period rather than the actual last populated value).

Has anyone been able to successfully make this situation work?

Thank you, in advance, for any assistance you can provide.

entry.title: Unable to consume from an OData V4 service using CAP Java
entry.content:
I created a full stack app in CAP using JAVA to consume from an existing OData service. I can consume from the standard business partner API by creating a projection on it. But, when I did the same thing for an OData V4 service( both custom OData V4 deployed in BTP and standard OData V4 service like Product, Bank), I get a 404 error when I tried to access the service. I have created custom handlers to handle the on read logic too but still get the same error.

Sample code using the Northwind OData V4 service

using { NorthWind } from './external/NorthWind';

entity my.app.Product as select from NorthWind.Products {
key ID,
@readonly Name,
@readonly Description,
@readonly Rating,
@readonly Price
};

@Path: 'nwind'
service NWindService {
entity Product as projection on my.app.Product;
}

Handler logic for read

import org.springframework.beans.factory.annotation.Qualifier;
import org.springframework.stereotype.Component;import com.sap.cds.Result;
import com.sap.cds.ql.Select;
import com.sap.cds.ql.cqn.CqnAnalyzer;
import com.sap.cds.ql.cqn.CqnSelect;
import com.sap.cds.reflect.CdsModel;
import com.sap.cds.services.cds.CdsReadEventContext;
import com.sap.cds.services.handler.EventHandler;
import com.sap.cds.services.handler.annotations.On;
import com.sap.cds.services.handler.annotations.ServiceName;

import cds.gen.northwind.NorthWind;
import cds.gen.northwind.NorthWind_;
import cds.gen.nwindservice.Product_;
import cds.gen.nwindservice.NWindService_;

@Component
@ServiceName(NWindService_.CDS_NAME)
public class NWindServiceHandler implements EventHandler {
private final NorthWind bupa;
private final CqnAnalyzer analyzer;
NWindServiceHandler(@Qualifier(NorthWind_.CDS_NAME) NorthWind bupa, CdsModel model) {
this.bupa = bupa;
this.analyzer = CqnAnalyzer.create(model);
}
@On(entity = Product_.CDS_NAME)
Result readProduct(CdsReadEventContext context) {
        Result products = bupa.run(context.getCqn());
return products;
}
}
 
application.yaml
---
spring:
  config.activate.on-profile: sandbox
cds:
  remote.services:
    '[NorthWind]':
      type: "odata-v4"
      destination:
        properties:
          url: "https://services.odata.org/experimental/OData/OData.svc/"
 
Error:

Exception marked the ChangeSet 4 as cancelled: The remote OData service responded with status code '404' (service 'NorthWind', event 'READ', entity 'NorthWind.Products')

2025-02-27T19:56:33.766Z ERROR 90246 --- [nio-8080-exec-3] c.s.c.a.o.v4.processors.CdsProcessor     : The remote OData service responded with status code '404' (service 'NorthWind', event 'READ', entity 'NorthWind.Products')

entry.title: Re: How to run an alvGrid in mb51 using BDC?
entry.content:
BDC is an obsolete term. It's been called "Batch Input" for a long time.

311440 - Batch input and controls - SAP for Me says:

Frontend "controls" (e.g. ALV Grid) are generally not capable of batch input...

entry.title: The Looming Tariff Tsunami: Is Your Business Ready?
entry.content:
“Tariff” is fast becoming the word of the year.

And the treat of tariffs is for sure the most talked about topic in boardrooms all over the world as they become “the hammer” in many geopolitical conversations!

Why?

Because over the past decades we have built often complex supply chains that span continents, delivering goods with incredible speed and competitive prices. However, this complexity also introduces vulnerabilities, and recently, tariffs have emerged as a major disruptor.

The Ripple Effect of Tariffs

When tariffs hit, the impact reverberates throughout the entire supply chain. To make any profit at all, importers need to pass some (if not all) of the increased costs onto customers, often leading to reduced demand. Retailers are then forced to rethink sourcing strategies, re-evaluate supplier relationships, and overhaul inventory management practices. The numbers can be staggering, with some estimates suggesting potential annual cost increases of thousands of dollars for consumers.

Adding to the challenge, businesses are already facing rising administrative costs (which have risen 14% from 2023-2024), regulatory pressures and the looming uncertainty of future trade policies. Leaders are actively seeking ways to mitigate these overhead expenses. While options like nearshoring or exploring new sourcing routes might seem appealing, they often prove to be short-term fixes with their own set of risks.

Beyond Reactive Measures: Embracing Technology-Driven Resilience

Instead of simply reacting to tariff changes, companies need a more proactive, long-term strategy. This is where technology, particularly advanced supply chain management solutions, comes into play. By harnessing the power of end-to-end data visibility, artificial intelligence (AI), and automation, businesses can transform their supply chains into resilient assets. They can not only withstand disruptions but emerge stronger and more adaptable:

  • Scenario Planning: Model "what-if" scenarios to test different supply chain strategies before implementation. By developing contingency plans to address potential disruptions through alternate sourcing regions, logistics routes, and pricing models you can minimize tariff exposure.
  • Alternative Sourcing Strategies: Because tariffs depend on country of origin, many companies will seek alternative suppliers in regions not impacted. In fact, nearly one-third of businesses are pursuing a dual supply chain approach where they have both global and local options. Business Network collaboration platforms not only help identify potential suppliers at risk but also provide alternative sources of supply and collaborative tools to ensure rapid and precise management of disruptions.
  • Inventory Optimization: Identifying key products that can be purchased in advance of tariffs to bolster inventory and ensuring that they are positioned in key locations across the supply chain will help minimize the impact of looming tariffs. Studies show that top-performing supply chain organizations are investing in AI and machine learning at twice the rate of their lower-performing peers.
  • Enhanced Supply Chain Visibility: Real-time tracking of demand fluctuations, inventory levels, and logistical operations, allowing businesses to swiftly adjust their strategies in response to new tariffs or other disruptions. This allows you to diversify suppliers, optimize inventory placement, and adjust logistics to minimize the impact of tariffs.

A Smarter Path Forward

In this volatile trade environment, resilience, agility, and adaptability are no longer optional – they're essential. By embracing the right technology, businesses can maintain competitiveness and ensure that tariffs don't dictate their success.

Discover how SAP Business Suite can help your business be more resilient to business challenges and responsive to opportunities.

Follow me on Twitter.

entry.title: HR integration active along with the Create Employee option in Maintain Employee FIORI Ap
entry.content:
Is it possible to view "Create Employee" button in the FIORI App "Maintain Employee" when the HR Business Partner Integration Switch(HRALX-HRAC) is still active? 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: HR integration active along with the Create Employee...
entry.content:
It is not possible.

To have HR integration active (employees replicated into S/4 from external source) and to create employees locally via "Maintain Employees" are mutually exclusive. Both cannot be used at the same time.

Documents like SAP note 3385503, below, approach this subject:

3385503 - Create button missing in Maintain Employees app

entry.title: abap bug MESSAGE shows wrong ID/class/number and navigate to wrong source position
entry.content:
I've got an ABAP program which sends a message with MESSAGE 'some text' TYPE 'E'.

Bug 1: when clicking the message to display a popup with the detailed information of the message, its content is wrong, there should be no message number and text should be "Character String as Message".

Bug 2: the button used to navigate to the ABAP source code position where the message is sent, goes to the wrong place (in fact it goes to the wrong message number/long text shown in the popup).

Any idea what the problem is and how to fix?

Thanks.

Sandra

Below are the program and function module to reproduce the bug.

Program:

 

REPORT.
PARAMETERS bug    RADIOBUTTON GROUP rb1 DEFAULT 'X'.
PARAMETERS no_bug RADIOBUTTON GROUP rb1.
AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN.
  CASE 'X'.
    WHEN no_bug.
      MESSAGE 'Message shown and correct detail popup shows "Character String as Message" and ABAP source code shown is this line' TYPE 'E'.
    WHEN bug.
      DATA(return) = VALUE string( ).
      CALL FUNCTION 'Z_ZSRO_MESSAGE_RAISING'
        IMPORTING  return = return
        EXCEPTIONS error  = 1.
      MESSAGE return TYPE 'E'.
  ENDCASE.

 

Function module:

 

FUNCTION Z_ZSRO_MESSAGE_RAISING
  EXPORTING
    RETURN TYPE STRING
  EXCEPTIONS
    ERROR.

  MESSAGE e398(00) WITH 'Message shown but buggy detail popup shows' 'TD600 and ABAP source code shown is' 'MESSAGE e600(td)' INTO return.

  MESSAGE e600(td) WITH '*' '*' '*' RAISING error.
ENDFUNCTION.

 

ABAP 7.40 SP 23.

Kernel 753 SP 1300.

SAP GUI 8.00.

entry.title: Re: abap bug MESSAGE shows wrong ID/class/number and navi...
entry.content:
A bug for sure.

Here's one workaround which consists in sending a dummy message right before "MESSAGE 'text' TYPE ..." statement:

 

REPORT.
PARAMETERS bug    RADIOBUTTON GROUP rb1 DEFAULT 'X'.
PARAMETERS no_bug RADIOBUTTON GROUP rb1.
AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN.
  CASE 'X'.
    WHEN no_bug.
      MESSAGE 'Message shown and correct detail popup shows "Character String as Message" and ABAP source code shown is this line' TYPE 'E'.
    WHEN bug.
      DATA(return) = VALUE string( ).
      CALL FUNCTION 'Z_ZSRO_MESSAGE_RAISING'
        IMPORTING  return = return
        EXCEPTIONS error  = 1.
      zcl_zzsro=>dummy_message_to_fix_bug( ). "<=========== Add this
      MESSAGE return TYPE 'E'.
  ENDCASE.

 

Class/Method code:

 

CLASS zcl_zzsro DEFINITION
  PUBLIC FINAL
  CREATE PRIVATE.

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    CLASS-METHODS dummy_message_to_fix_bug.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_zzsro IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD dummy_message_to_fix_bug.
    " This is to fix the bug described in this post:
    " https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/abap-bug-message-shows-wrong-id-class-number-and-navigate-to-wrong-source/qaq-p/14033754
    MESSAGE s001(00) INTO DATA(dummy) ##NEEDED.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

 

After this fix, the popup shows the right information, and the right ABAP source code position is shown:

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Planning Copy from Upload Version to Public Version: Overwrite issue
entry.content:
Hi, 

For better control end result - Advanced Formulas step is the best. 

For standard copy step it's designed behavior (in Advanced Formulas step the same setting is CONFIG.GENERATE_UNBOOKED_DATA = ON)

If you want to save existing values and overwrite only new uploaded rows use Advanced formulas step with setting: CONFIG.GENERATE_UNBOOKED_DATA = OFF

More info could be found here: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/afe93e3cf1414a7b8419baad11cc066e.html?locale=en-US

Best regards,

entry.title: Tablet won't display PDF inside of an item without opening a new window or PDF viewer
entry.content:
When I add a PDF to an item with an AICC wrapper, it works fine on PC. However, when taking the item on mobile tablet, it prompts to download the PDF and open in another tab or PDF viewer app. I would like it to display natively inside of the same tab. 

More Info

  • Tablet: Zebra Technologies ET51 running Android Version 11
  • Using Microsoft Edge browser, but does the same thing in Chrome

entry.title: crystal reports 2016: merge data across two (BTrieve) databases
entry.content:
We are trying to rewrite an ancient CR 8.5 report that merges data from two BTrieve databases and it works fine, but we need to convert it to CR 2016. 

The old report used a table of "months" as the main table and that table is joined to each of the two "real" data tables, which are transactions (TRX).  One of the TRX tables resides in the same database as the "months" table and the other TRX table resides in the other database. With this structure, the report doesn't complain about "More than one datasource has been used in this report."   

However, we cannot get the same functionality in CR 2016 when going against BTrieve data. It always complains about "more than one datasource" and the data returned is a cartesian product.

We can create stored procedures and views in the BTrieve database that gives us the data using a UNION (SQL), but the BTrieve driver in CR 2016 does not allow access to stored procedures or views (sigh).

Anyone, have any ideas on a solution/workaround?

NOTE:  The application that performs all the processing and drives the reports does not use ODBC which does allow access to stored procedures and views in BTrieve files.

Thanks.     -- Paul

SAP Crystal Reports

2. Step 2 would be to create either a "Repeat" loop or "Forever" loop based on whether a set number of times execution is needed or based on an exit condition the loop needs to be ended.

3. Step 3: Within the loop address each set activity for the three collections, and add the index of Step 2 loop to address the element on the current loop index.

Do let me know in case you wish for more details.

Best Regards,

Rohit

entry.title: SQL Anywhere Linux ODBC and TLS
entry.content:
I am setting up a Docker container to connect to a SQL Anywhere database running on the same network. I was able to get it to connect and run a query successfully when I ran the Docker container on the same machine as the database server. I am using the sql Anywhere Linux ODBC client software and unixodbc.

This is the odbc.ini that works when the container is ran on the same machine:

 

[ODBC Data Sources]
Prototype = SQL Anywhere 17

[Prototype]
Driver=/opt/sqlanywhere17/lib64/libdbodbc17.so
Description=Some Database
Host=192.168.1.11:2638
DatabaseName=databasename
ServerName=servername
UID=username
Integrated=NO
Encryption=SIMPLE
Password=password

 

Here is how the database is sarted:

 

[HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\SQLANYs_testdb\Parameters]
"Parameters"="-k -qi -qs -on 10m -o C:\\CPDB\\console.txt -ti240 -tl120 -gnh 500 -gl ALL -gd ALL -x tcpip(ServerPort=2636,2638) -xs HTTPS(PORT=2637;IDENTITY=c:\\somefolder\\odbc.id;IDENTITY_PASSWORD=test) -es -ec SIMPLE,TLS(IDENTITY=c:\\somefolder\\odbc.id;IDENTITY_PASSWORD=test) -n test C:\\CPDB\\test.db @c:\\somefolder\\.tekey"

 

I am assuming that this works because it is on the same machine and it uses Encryption=SIMPLE. When I run this Docker image on a different machine on the same network with this odbc.ini:

 

[ODBC Data Sources]
Prototype = SQL Anywhere 17

[Prototype]
Driver=/opt/sqlanywhere17/lib64/libdbodbc17.so
Description=Some Database
Host=192.168.1.11:2638
DatabaseName=databasename
ServerName=servername
UID=username
Integrated=NO
Encryption=TLS(trusted_certificates=*)
Password=password

 

When I run:

 

ODBCTRACE=1 ODBCTRACEMODE=console isql -v Prototype

 

I get:

 

[08S01][unixODBC][SAP][ODBC Driver][SQL Anywhere]TLS handshake failure
[ISQL]ERROR: Could not SQLConnect

 

Here is what the public key looks like minus sensitive details:

 

Certificate:
    Data:
        Version: 3 (0x2)
        Serial Number:
            <omitted>
        Signature Algorithm: sha256WithRSAEncryption
        Issuer: C=US, ST=UT, L=Somewhere, O=Org, OU=Database, CN=*.somecompany.com, emailAddress=DatabaseTeam@test.com
        Validity
            Not Before: Feb 28 17:52:02 2024 GMT
            Not After : Feb 15 17:52:02 2074 GMT
        Subject: C=US, ST=UT, L=Somewhere, O=Org, OU=Database, CN=*.somecompany.com, emailAddress=DatabaseTeam@test.com
        Subject Public Key Info:
            Public Key Algorithm: rsaEncryption
                Public-Key: (2048 bit)
                Modulus:
                    <omitted>
                Exponent: 65537 (0x10001)
        X509v3 extensions:
            X509v3 Subject Key Identifier: 
                <omitted>
    Signature Algorithm: sha256WithRSAEncryption
    Signature Value:
        <omitted>

 


Where can I find the different options that can be used for TLS(...) in the odbc.ini file ?

And does anyone have any suggestions on how I can troubleshoot this ? I think I am just not getting the TLS configured correctly in the odbc.ini.

entry.title: Re: difference in asset report & g.l account balance
entry.content:
Hi All, I had the same issue and I took sometime to fix it.

I then understood that, asset reports work based on asset value date and GL reports based on posting date.

It is worth checking if any settlement was done (example, cost object to AUC or FA) with posting date previous month but AstValue date (BSEG-BZDAT) in current month. 

To obtain all docs for these asset posting, I had checked first BSEG table with BUKRS, GJAHR and BZDAT (note, value and ast value dates are different). then passing all these to BKPF, I understood, only two docs type PS and AA. So I then run BKPF with posting date of these two months to know if any postings are falling under Asset value date of different month. Do not forget that BSEG has no plus /minus sign so consider D/C-S/H indicator while totaling or do subtotal on D/C. It resolved my issue.

Thanks.

Sachin.

entry.title: Re: CK13N different time values are displayed
entry.content: we can see 72442 - Unit conversion in product costing The unit of measurement used is that of the activity type, for example, H. The system now attempts to convert all the units of measurement to H. However, there are two exceptions: 1. If the value exceeds the field size (field overflow), the next highest unit of measurement is used, for example, DAY. 2. If the conversion causes rounding errors higher than one thousandth, for example, 29 MIN = 0.483333...H, the next lowest unit of measurement (in this case, MIN) is used. See Note 62919 for more information.

entry.title: Re: Create one or more projects for Migration Cockpit for...
entry.content:
Hello Ebba,

It's not necessary to create only one migration project for all your rollouts, especially the rollouts will take 2 years to complete. The migration object templates will probably change during the 2 years, then you will have to update the templates, which makes you difficult to maintain and track the status.

So, you can create separate migration project for each rollout. And you can even create several migration projects during one rollout. The field mappings are based on project level, meaning you don't need to maintain the field mapping again within the same project. If you create a new migration project, you need to maintain the field mapping. That's the main difference.

Thanks

Vincent

entry.title: 台湾地区统一发票在 SAP ERP 公有云系统中的实施方案建议
entry.content:
在数字化时代，电子发票已成为企业提升流程效率和合规的重要工具。作为专注于 SAP ERP 公有云的解决方案架构师，基于丰富的项目参与经验，本文提供关于台湾地区统一发票管理的深入洞见，旨在帮助企业用户充分利用 SAP 产品实现统一发票的数字化管理。

 

电子发票流程的必要性

根据台湾财政部的法规，电子发票已被推荐作为统一发票标准格式。SAP ERP 公有云产品提供强大的支持，成为企业在数字化转型中不可或缺的组成部分。在众多实施项目中，企业面临的挑战通常在于如何有效整合电子发票到现有系统及流程中。为确保从纸质到电子系统的过渡顺利，建议采用分阶段实施策略。

 

理解并优化现有发票流程

在许多企业中，纸质发票的管理常常带来操作上的繁琐以及高错误率。当企业定制化的系统流程与功能中涉及纸质发票流程时，可能会遇到以下问题：

  • 整合难度：纸质和电子发票在系统层面的整合可能涉及多个系统之间的信息同步和数据转换，增加了技术复杂性。
  • 效率低下：处理纸质发票通常需要人工干预，如扫描和手动数据输入，这可能导致效率降低。
  • 错误风险：手动过程中容易出现数据错误，可能导致财务报表的不准确。

建议企业选择分开管理纸质和电子发票，并逐步推广至全电子化进程。基于对 SAP ERP 公有云的深入认识，系统中的严谨配置和测试至关重要，以防止发生发票号码等关键数据整合问题。

 

处理停用的电子计算机统一发票

自2021年以来，电子计算机统一发票已经停止适用，参考台湾当地财政部税务署公告。停止适用后，为了管理和查阅历史交易数据，SAP 系统中仍可能出现与电子计算机发票相关的税码设置。为了防止错误使用停用的税码，项目顾问需要利用税条件类型的设置，限制用户只能选择当前实际适用的销项税码。将客户税分类码进行调整，如用“E”来对应电子发票税码，以替代传统设计的“1”税码，是一个有效的解决方案。

 

电子发票功能的全面展望

SAP ERP 公有云提供一整套电子发票标准税码，以协助企业满足多样化的税务需求：

  • A5：零税率销售，如出口商品。
  • A6：针对境内一般课税。
  • A7：境内零税。
  • A8：境内免税/免开立电子发票销售。
  • A9：用于境内特殊税率。

在设计和实施这些功能时，建议企业激活 5XU 范围项目，并参考以下的配置指南，以确保最大化利用这一数字化工具：

  • 客户（销售）电子发票相关配置指南
  • 电子发票类型与编码配置指南
  • VAT 申报配置指南
  • 营业场所配置指南

 

预备未来的发票标准

根据电子发票数据交换标准信息构建指引(MIG)，从2026年起，电子发票需遵循 MIG4.1 标准。当前，SAP 的系统版本支持 MIG4.0 标准，产品研发团队已经积极应对更新法规的要求，未来的系统更新已列入日程。建议企业提前规划，以便顺利适应法规变更。

 

免开立电子发票的场景处理

依据统一发票使用办法第四条，某些免税商品可免开立统一发票。在这些情况下，SAP 标准流程并不会自动阻止电子发票的生成。因此，项目可通过增强 BADI EDOC_TW_IS_RELEVANT 实现定制化逻辑，以条件性地抑制电子发票的生成，从而满足业务需求。

 

多元税码场景的应对方法

随着 MIG4.0 标准的启动，“F0401 平台存证开立发票”可在同一张凭证中包含应税与免税或零税率的项目。SAP ERP 公有云自 2502 版本起支持混合税率的电子发票。这意味着，在同一张凭证中可以包含应税与免税或零税率的项目。企业可以利用此功能实现更多样的发票处理模式。详细说明参见 Multiple Tax | SAP What's New Viewer。

但若企业管理的需求仍需对不同税率单独开票，有两种方案可预期实现：

  • 流程控制：规范操作流程，确保同一销售订单仅限使用同一税率，从根源上预防混合税率情况。
  • 技术增强：使用 BADI SD_BIL_DATA_TRANSFER 实施定制化逻辑，在销售请款凭证生成时，按税码拆分请款凭证。需注意，根据 SAP 的设计，电子发票凭证需与销售请款凭证保持一一对应关系。详细参见 Forcing a Billing Document Split。

 

集成加值中心与 VAT 申报

在某些项目中，部分企业选择通过加值中心进行 VAT 申报，这涉及如何将 SAP 系统的电子发票数据有效推送至增值中心。由于不同加值中心对电子信息交互的要求略有差异，SAP 无法提供统一的解决方案符合所有加值中心的要求，因此需定制化开发以匹配不同加值中心的技术需求，确保准确数据传输与合规。

项目实施可通过销售流程凭证的相关 CDS 视图读取数据，并整合为电子发票申报集成所需的数据格式。以下博客详细阐述了一个实施实例：嵌入式 SAC 客户用例分享之加值中心集成（台湾地区本地化需求）。若您期望 SAP 能通过标准产品内容提供统合的台湾电子发票 CDS 视图，欢迎您通过贵司的 S-User 访问 Release Unified CDS Views for Taiwan e-Invoice Data Export and Integrations | SAP Customer Influence Portal，投出您宝贵的一票。



结语

通过这些洞见，企业在统一发票的数字化管理旅程中能够获得新的视角和实用策略。成功的电子发票应用促进业务流程改进，提升合规性，也是企业数字化中不可忽视的环节。欲进一步详细了解 SAP 台湾电子发票标准方案，欢迎参考SAP Document Compliance 台湾电子发票解决方案。

entry.title: What Is SAP Business Data Cloud? Benefits, Architecture & Use Cases
entry.content:
Recently, SAP announced a new solution that represents an evolution in SAP's data strategy – SAP Business Data Cloud. In this blog, we will try to understand SAP Business Data Cloud in simple layman’s terms.

We will understand:

  • Why is SAP Business Data Cloud needed?
  • What exactly is SAP Business Data Cloud?
  • Major Benefits of SAP Business Data Cloud?
  • Architecture of SAP Business Data Cloud
  • And many more.

Disclaimer: All the views and opinions in the blog are my own and is made in my personal capacity and that SAP shall not be responsible or liable for any of the contents published in this blog.

Let’s start! 

Before understanding what SAP Business Data Cloud is, you should understand why it is needed.  

 

Why is SAP Business Data Cloud needed?

SAP customers use many different SAP and non-SAP systems to store their data. This creates lots of data silos. These data, stored in separate places makes it hard to connect.

For example,

  • A company using SAP S/4HANA for finance and SAP SuccessFactors for HR may struggle to combine employee salary and performance data for analysis.
  • A retail company using SAP Sales Cloud and few other non-SAP solutions may find it difficult to harmonize their data and link customer purchases with marketing campaigns.

SAP Business Data Cloud combines new and existing technologies to provide customers with

  • curated and enriched data products
  • advanced analytics and cross-analytics,
  • benchmarking data,
  • and AI-powered Insights Applications that enable smart decisions and optimize business outcomes.

 

Another important point is related to the rise of AI. With AI being focus of every innovation, companies need an advanced solution, which not only centralizes data from various SAP and non-SAP sources, but also provides new dimension of insights, advanced analytics, and AI capabilities. – This is what SAP Business Data Cloud provides.

 

So, what exactly is SAP Business Data Cloud?

In simple words:

SAP Business Data Cloud is a new product from SAP that helps businesses store, manage, and connect data in one place. It makes data easy to use and understand.

  • Technically, SAP Business Data Cloud is an SAP managed SaaS solution.
  • It runs on SAP BTP.
  • It centralizes data from SAP and non-SAP sources into a unified semantic layer.
  • It provides a new dimension of insights, advanced analytics, and AI capabilities.

 

SAP Business Data Cloud combines strategic solutions such as SAP HANA Cloud, SAP Datasphere, and SAP Analytics Cloud and their capabilities in one single solution.

 

What makes SAP Business Data Cloud so Unique?

What makes SAP Business Data Cloud so unique, is that it offers the tools and technologies to meet all data and analytics requirements of a modern and agile organization.

It uses the latest technology to support scenarios such as:

  • Out-of-the-box reporting.
  • Machine learning and artificial intelligence.
  • Advanced data modeling and data warehousing.
  • Powerful planning and reporting.
  • Intelligent data management.

 

SAP Business Data Cloud covers almost all the requirements of a modern data and analytics solution and thus serves different target audiences with different requirements.

 

SAP Business Data Cloud Architecture

SAP Business Data Cloud system landscape consists of different interconnected components as shown in below diagram.

 

Source Systems

In SAP Business Data Cloud, Source Systems refer to the data providers that can be connected to bring data for integration, modeling, and analytics. These sources include SAP systems such as SAP S/4HANA Cloud or SAP SuccessFactors as well as non-SAP systems.

 

Foundation Service

The Foundation Service is the backbone of SAP Business Data Cloud. It provides core capabilities for seamless data access, governance, security, and integration across different data sources.

  • When analysis is required, the bundled data from source system is replicated into the Foundation Services.
  • This data is then harmonized with other relevant business data from other business applications, transformed, and enriched to become a Data Product.
  • The data products are stored in a hyperscaler environment based on SAP HANA Cloud, Data Lake files and is ready to be used.

 

SAP Business Data Cloud cockpit

The SAP Business Data Cloud cockpit provides tools to:

  • browse and find pre-delivered Insight Apps and Data Products
  • install Insight Apps
  • share Data Products with SAP Databricks to be able to extend them with AI capabilities

 

SAP Datasphere

SAP Datasphere is the heart of SAP Business Data Cloud. It provides the foundational structures that define the data model on top of the Data Products.

This includes pre-delivered Insight App and Data Product scenarios but also scenarios with custom data models that can be manually extended with AI.

SAP Datasphere also serves as a central tool for managing analytical roles and data access control.

 

SAP Databricks

SAP Databricks is used to extend analytics with functions that are based on AI and machine learning.

There are different approaches to achieve this.

We can share the Data Products from SAP Business Data Cloud Catalog with SAP Databricks, to extend them accordingly and to push them back to SAP Business Data Cloud Catalog.

Other option is to share data directly from SAP Databricks into SAP Business Data Cloud Catalog.

In both cases, your data models can be extended to enrich classic analytics with insights that are usually implemented by Data Scientists.

 

SAP BW or SAP BW/4HANA

SAP BW is integrated into SAP Business Data Cloud if you use the SAP BW Netweaver Cloud, private edition or the SAP BW/4HANA Cloud, private edition.

The approach makes it possible to technically onboard SAP BW objects into SAP Business Data Cloud, allowing them to be used as Data Products.

 

SAP Analytics Cloud

SAP Analytics Cloud stories are used to provide the required dashboard in out-of-the-box reporting scenarios with Insight Apps.

  • SAP Analytics Cloud serves as a central tool for exploring the requested business insights or executing planning functions.
  • Further, business users can use it to run ad-hoc analysis, explore the provided data with AI-driven chat capabilities, or act on their insights with AI-driven suggestions.

 

Insight Apps

In addition to SAP Analytics Cloud, it is planned to provide SAP BTP based Insight Apps. These SAP managed SaaS applications are delivered in SAP Business Data Cloud to focus on various action-driven scenarios such as analytics, prediction, or forecasting.

 

What is the importance of SAP Business Data Cloud in the field of AI?

Even the best AI tools are only as good as the data they are trained with. If a Large Language Model can’t access data, it can’t learn from it. And this is one of the main challenges for enterprises today.

With SAP Business Data Cloud, SAP is enabling organizations access to a high-quality shared data based on one single source of truth. BDC breaks down data silos and unifies SAP and non-SAP, structured and unstructured data, to create one common semantical data layer that gives a truly holistic understanding of your business.

 

What is Data Product in SAP Business Data Cloud?

Let’s assume a company operating in multiple countries needs to analyze regional sales performance, customer distribution, and tax regulations. However, the relevant data is scattered across different tables in SAP S/4HANA.

Traditionally, to analyze the data:

  • We need to extract and join data from multiple tables.
  • Multiple teams request separate extracts, leading to duplicate efforts.
  • There is no unified governance, increasing the risk of inconsistencies.

 

In other words - To analyze data from different SAP sources, we need to extract, transform, merge, and load those data into different analytical applications. It also requires lots of effort and skill.

To solve this problem, SAP Business Data Cloud comes with a concept of Data Product.

Data Products are:

  • Operational data with predefined and stable structure and interfaces
  • predefined by SAP for common use cases (e.g., sales, finance, HR).
  • can be extended/customized based on business needs
  • can be consumed in an easy, trustful, and secure way
 

 

For example, SAP has provided a data product called “CountryRegion”. With the "CountryRegion" Data Product in Business Data Cloud, we can:

  • Easily unifies country and region-related data from S/4HANA
  • Enriches it with business logic, tax rates, and compliance details.
  • Secures it with role-based access for different teams.
  • Makes it available for reporting.
  •  

The major benefit of Data Product is that..

With Data Products, we only have to consume the data provided and adapt it to our specific use cases.

All the extraction, harmonization, and joining of the different tables has been done for us by the Data Product provider.

Any changes to the data preparation behind the scenes are then the responsibility of the Data Product owner.

 

Got any further query? Let me know in comment. 

References

https://news.sap.com/2025/02/sap-business-data-cloud-databricks-turbocharge-business-ai/

Introducing SAP Business Data Cloud

 

Liked it? Don't forget to give a kudos!

entry.title: Expert Insights on Adopting Solutions for Taiwan's Government Uniform Invoices (GUI)
entry.content:
In the digital era, electronic invoicing has become an important tool for enterprises to improve process efficiency and compliance. As a solution architect focusing on SAP ERP public cloud, based on rich project participation experience, by this blog I'd like to provide in-depth insights into Taiwan Government Uniform Invoice (GUI) management, aiming to help enterprise users fully utilize SAP products to achieve unified invoice digital management.

 

Necessity of Electronic Invoice Processes

According to Taiwan Ministry of Finance regulations, electronic invoices have been recommended as a standard format. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition provide strong support, becoming an indispensable component in the digital transformation of enterprises. In numerous implementation projects, the challenge for enterprises usually lies in effectively integrating electronic invoices into existing systems and processes. To ensure a smooth transition from paper to electronic systems, a phased implementation strategy is recommended.

 

Understanding and Optimizing Existing Invoice Processes

In many enterprises, the management of paper invoices often leads to operational complexity and high error rates. When custom system processes and functions involve paper invoice processes, the following issues may occur:

  • Integration difficulty: Integration of paper and electronic invoice systems may involve information synchronization and data conversion between multiple systems, increasing technical complexity.
  • Low efficiency: Processing paper invoices often requires manual intervention, such as scanning and manual data input, which may reduce efficiency.
  • Error risk: Manual processes are prone to data errors, which may lead to inaccuracies in financial statements.

It is recommended that enterprises manage paper and electronic invoices separately and gradually transition to a fully electronic process. Based on an in-depth understanding of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, meticulous system configuration and testing are crucial to prevent key data integration issues such as invoice numbers.

 

Handling of Disabled Computerized Uniform Invoices

Since 2021, computerized uniform invoices have been discontinued. Refer to Taiwan local Ministry of Finance Taxation Bureau announcement. After discontinuation, for managing and reviewing historical transaction data, SAP systems may still include tax code settings related to computerized invoices. To prevent incorrect use of discontinued tax codes, project consultants need to use tax condition type settings, restricting users to select only currently applicable output tax codes. Adjusting customer tax classification codes, such as using "E" to correspond to electronic invoice tax codes to replace the traditionally designed "1" tax code, is an effective solution.

Comprehensive Outlook on Electronic Invoice Functions

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition provides a complete set of standard electronic invoice tax codes to help enterprises meet diverse tax needs:

  • A5: Zero tax rate sales, such as export goods.
  • A6: General taxation within Taiwan region.
  • A7: Zero tax within Taiwan region.
  • A8: Tax-exempt/sales w/o issuing electronic invoices withinTaiwan region.
  • A9: Used for special tax rates withinTaiwan region.

In designing and implementing these functions, it is recommended that enterprises activate the 5XU scope item and refer to the following configuration guides to maximize the use of this digital tool:

  • Customer (Sales) Electronic Invoice Configuration Guide
  • Electronic Invoice Type and Coding Configuration Guide
  • VAT Reporting Configuration Guide
  • Business Place Configuration Guide

 

Preparing for Future Invoice Standards

According to Electronic Invoice Data Exchange Standard Message Implementation Guide (MIG), from 2026 onwards, electronic invoices must comply with the MIG4.1 standard. The current SAP system version supports MIG4.0 standard, and the product development team is actively responding to the requirements of updated regulations, with future system updates already scheduled. Enterprises are advised to plan ahead to smoothly adapt to regulatory changes.

 

Handling Scenarios Exempt from Issuing Electronic Invoices

According to the 4th item of Taiwan Government Uniform Invoice Usage Regulations, some tax-exempt goods can be exempt from issuing GUI. In these cases, the SAP standard process does not automatically prevent the generation of electronic invoices. Thus, projects can achieve custom logic through enhancing BADI EDOC_TW_IS_RELEVANT to conditionally suppress the generation of electronic invoices, thereby meeting business needs.

 

Handling Mix Tax Codes Scenarios

With the activation of the MIG4.0 standard, "F0401 platform certificate invoice" can contain taxable and tax-exempt or zero-rated items in the same certificate. With the release of 2502, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition supports mixed tax rate electronic invoices. This means that taxable and tax-exempt or zero-rated items can be included in the same invoice. Enterprises can utilize this functionality to achieve more diverse invoice processing modes. For more detailed instructions, refer to Multiple Tax | SAP What's New Viewer.

However, if the enterprise's management needs still require separate invoicing for different tax rates, there are two workaround solutions:

  • Process control: Standardize the business operation process to ensure that the same sales order is limited to using the same tax rate, preventing the situation of mixed tax rates from the source.
  • Technical enhancement: Apply customized logic using BADI SD_BIL_DATA_TRANSFER to split billing documents by tax code when generating sales billing documents. Note that, according to SAP's design, electronic invoice documents need to maintain a one-to-one correspondence with sales billing documents. For more details, refer to Forcing a Billing Document Split.

 

Integrating Value-added Center with VAT Reporting

In certain projects, some enterprises choose to conduct VAT reporting through value-added centers, which involves how to effectively push electronic invoice data from the SAP system to the value-added center. Since different value-added centers have slightly differing requirements for electronic information interaction, SAP cannot provide a unified solution. Therefore, custom development is required to match the technical needs of different value-added centers, ensuring accurate data transmission and compliance.

Project implementation can read data through the relevant CDS views of the sales/billing documents and integrate them into the data format required for electronic invoice reporting integration. The following blog details an implementation example: Embedded SAC Customer Case Sharing on Value-added Center Integration (Taiwan Region Localization Needs). If you expect SAP to provide unified Taiwan electronic invoice CDS views through standard product content, we welcome you to visit Release Unified CDS Views for Taiwan e-Invoice Data Export and Integrations | SAP Customer Influence Portal with your company's S-User and cast your valuable vote.



Conclusion

Through these insights, enterprises can gain new perspectives and practical strategies in the digital management journey of unified invoices. Successful electronic invoice applications promote business process improvements and enhance compliance, which are crucial aspects of enterprise digitalization. To further understand SAP's Taiwan electronic invoice standard solutions, you are welcome to refer to SAP Document Compliance Taiwan e-Invoice Solution.

entry.title: SHDB transaction is not checking S_BDC_MONI
entry.content:
In my company there are some systems that allow me to execute SHDB to do recording and make any changes to other user recording without having the object S_BDC_MONI. But there are some systems as well that did check the object and does not allow to perform anything on the SHDB screen.

In the program level of the SHDB how can I find if it is checking if user have S_BDC_MONI object before allow to do recording or any changes? 

Any SAP note that can make this consistence across all my company systems?

Fiori apps to create reference Functional Locations are not supported in Public Cloud (Tcodes IL11 etc). It looks like some of the other Web Dynpro apps do refer to them still (as per your screenshot) which could be confusing.

This has also been discussed in the following SAP note: 3009716 - Reference Location apps ( Create , Change , Display ) missing in S4HANA Cloud 

I also can't see that anyone has raised it as a Customer Influence request, so if you feel it needs to be added, please raise one: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Asset Management - All Requests 

Regards,

Tim

entry.title: Re: Joule Extensibility POC for SAP Learning
entry.content:
Joule can leverage advanced GenAI capabilities, document foundation, and both SAP and non-SAP data sources to develop custom skills1. This means that while Joule is primarily integrated with SAP Line of Business products, there is potential for extending its capabilities to other SAP platforms, such as the SAP Learning platform, through custom development.

Given this flexibility, it seems feasible to proceed with your proof of concept to implement a chatbot that enhances user experience on the SAP Learning platform. However, it would be prudent to consult with SAP support or a technical expert to ensure that all necessary integrations and customizations can be effectively implemented . Currently with limited information that's all we can suggest.

entry.title: Re: SAC interview questions
entry.content:
Hi,

please find below few Q&A.

 

  1. What is SAP Analytics Cloud? A: SAP Analytics Cloud is a cloud-based analytics solution that allows users to analyze data, create visualizations, and generate insights for better decision-making.

2 What are the key features of SAP Analytics Cloud? A: Some key features of SAP Analytics Cloud include data visualization, planning and forecasting, augmented analytics, collaboration, and integration with other SAP solutions.

 3 How is data imported into SAP Analytics Cloud? A: Data can be imported into SAP Analytics Cloud from various sources, including Excel files, CSV files, SQL databases, SAP HANA, and other cloud-based data sources.

4 What is a story in SAP Analytics Cloud? A: A story in SAP Analytics Cloud refers to a collection of visualizations, charts, and tables that are created to represent and analyze data in a meaningful way.

5 How can you create a model in SAP Analytics Cloud? A: To create a model in SAP Analytics Cloud, you need to connect to a data source, define the data structure, and map the fields to dimensions and measures.

6 How can you create calculations in SAP Analytics Cloud? A: Calculations can be created in SAP Analytics Cloud using formulas, scripting, or by using the calculation engine provided within the application.

7 What is the difference between a private and a public dimension in SAP Analytics Cloud? A: A private dimension in SAP Analytics Cloud is only visible within a specific story, while a public dimension is accessible across multiple stories.

8 How does data blending work in SAP Analytics Cloud? A: Data blending in SAP Analytics Cloud allows you to combine data from multiple sources into a single model for analysis and reporting.

9 What is Smart Discovery in SAP Analytics Cloud? A: Smart Discovery is a feature in SAP Analytics Cloud that uses machine learning algorithms to automatically detect patterns, correlations, and insights in your data.

10 How can you schedule data refresh in SAP Analytics Cloud? A: You can schedule data refresh in SAP Analytics Cloud by configuring a data source connection and specifying the frequency and time for automatic refresh.

11 What is the difference between a filter and a context filter in SAP Analytics Cloud? A: A filter in SAP Analytics Cloud allows you to restrict the data displayed based on specific conditions, while a context filter further optimizes performance by reducing the data set before applying other filters.

12 How can you share a story in SAP Analytics Cloud? A: You can share a story in SAP Analytics Cloud by granting access to specific users or user groups, or by publishing the story as a link that can be accessed by anyone with the URL.

13 Can you integrate SAP Analytics Cloud with other SAP solutions? A: Yes, SAP Analytics Cloud can be integrated with other SAP solutions such as SAP S/4HANA, SAP BW/4HANA, SAP BusinessObjects, and SAP Data Warehouse Cloud.

14 What is data wrangling in SAP Analytics Cloud? A: Data wrangling in SAP Analytics Cloud refers to the process of preparing and transforming data for analysis, including data cleansing, harmonization, and enrichment.

15 How can you create a forecast in SAP Analytics Cloud? A: You can create a forecast in SAP Analytics Cloud by selecting a measure, defining the forecast period and method, and running the forecasting algorithm.

16 What is the advantage of using Smart Insights in SAP Analytics Cloud? A: Smart Insights in SAP Analytics Cloud automatically highlights key patterns, trends, and outliers in your data, helping you identify important insights quickly.

17 How can you create a geo map in SAP Analytics Cloud? A: To create a geo map in SAP Analytics Cloud, you need to have location data (latitude and longitude) in your dataset, and then you can select the geo map visualization type and map the dimensions accordingly.

18 Can you explain the data modeling capabilities in SAP Analytics Cloud? A: SAP Analytics Cloud provides data modeling capabilities that allow you to define relationships between tables, create hierarchies, implement calculations, and apply filters to shape and structure your data.

19 How can you create a calculation view in SAP Analytics Cloud? A: To create a calculation view in SAP Analytics Cloud, you can use the Calculation Editor, where you define formulas and expressions to perform calculations and derive new measures based on existing data.

20 What is data storytelling, and how can it be achieved in SAP Analytics Cloud? A: Data storytelling involves using visualizations, annotations, and narratives to communicate insights derived from data. In SAP Analytics Cloud, you can create data stories by combining various visualizations, adding context, and providing explanations to guide the audience through the data.

 

Hope it helps.

regards

Giri
